32ab7.summary: Adds relation-depth expansion for architecture_context/component_rel export traversal while preserving the active tag boundary for all expanded nodes.
32ab7.content: Adds relation-depth expansion for architecture_context/component_rel export traversal while preserving the active tag boundary for all expanded nodes.

3ec07.summary: Suspicious session challenges are handled through explicit operational commands and a repo-local judge agent.
3ec07.content:
Suspicious session challenges are handled through explicit operational commands and a repo-local judge agent.

- `zhar session current` reports the active session and its challenge settings.
- `zhar session clear` deletes the active transient session file when operators need to reset runtime state.
- `.github/agents/challenge-judge.agent.md` defines the independent review workflow for suspicious nodes.

40d23.summary: The CLI now supports env-backed body input through both `--from-env NAME` and `--content-var NAME` on `add`, `note`, and `add-note`.
40d23.content:
The CLI now supports env-backed body input through both `--from-env NAME` and `--content-var NAME` on `add`, `note`, and `add-note`.

For non-memory-backed node types, `zhar add ... --from-env NAME` and `zhar add ... --content-var NAME` create an attached supplemental note automatically instead of dropping the staged body.

This surface reads environment variables only. In PowerShell, use `$env:NAME = ...`, not `$NAME = ...`.

48aa2.content:
## Update

Harness guidance now treats `zhar harness get --help` as the discoverability entry point and `zhar harness get <key>` as the authoritative mirrored runtime view from `src/zhar/harness/files/`.

## Workflow split

Repo-authored harness workflow now lives in `zhar-harness-workflow`, covering `.github/` edits, mirror sync, runtime verification, and `--check`.

Safe durable-memory mutation now lives in `zhar-memory-mutation`, covering read-state-first, owner-first routing, CLI mutation, and validation.

## Policy split

`zhar.agent.md` now points memory invariants and lifecycle rules back to `instruction-zhar-memory` instead of duplicating those sections inline.
